items.title: OIDC ou SAML : lequel vous faut-il vraiment
items.description: Toute équipe qui développe un logiciel B2B se heurte au même carrefour la première fois qu'un client sérieux annonce « il nous faut le SSO ». Deux acronymes, OIDC et SAML, prétendant chacun être la réponse, et un internet rempli de tableaux comparatifs qui vous disent que SAML est « entreprise »

items.title: Production Interceptors for Solon ReActAgent: Stop Loops, Retry Tools, Sanitize Observations, Stream Events
items.description:
Demo agents usually work once. Production agents fail in boring, expensive ways: they loop on the same tool call, they retry forever against a flaky API, and they paste a 40KB JSON blob back into the next thought.

Solon AI already ships a small set of built-in ReAct interceptors

items.title: My Mac Said “System Data” Was Using 300 GB. Here’s How I Actually Cleared It.
items.description:
I opened System Settings → General → Storage and stared at the screen for a second.

macOS: 47 GB. Fine.

System Data: 300 GB.

No delete button. No info icon. Just a giant mystery bucket eating my disk.

items.title: Instrument First, Then Prompt: Finding Real Agentic Pipeline Bugs
items.description:
The default reaction when an agentic pipeline misbehaves is to open the system prompt and start rewriting it. The instinct makes sense — the prompt is visible, editable, and feels like the thing you control.

The problem is that the prompt is almost never where the bug is.

What Act

items.title: 舌尖上的“神迹”：当一个吃货被塔可辣到灵魂出窍

前言：文本背景还原

请闭上眼睛，想象这样一个场景：凌晨两点半，在洛杉矶街头一个闪烁着霓虹灯的墨西哥卷饼摊前。一位狂热的食客刚刚吃下了一口涂满了魔鬼椒酱的塔可（Taco）。

items.title: I Asked AI to Turn One Sentence Into a Startup Blueprint — Here's What I Learned
items.description:
Like a lot of people who enjoy building things, I have a notebook full of startup ideas.

The problem isn't coming up with ideas.

The problem is knowing which ones are worth pursuing.
